query: central banks governors solidarity statement Jerome Powell January 2026
a_issues: A never includes the exact official ECB/central-bank statement page (the anchor) and relies entirely on secondary press coverage (Guardian, Reuters, Bloomberg, LA Times, Le Monde). It quotes only the opening sentence of the statement and gives an unverified figure of '10' signatories that conflicts with Le Monde's own claim of 'eleven' in the same result set, creating an internal inconsistency. Reuters/Bloomberg headlines also suggest the statement was still 'being worked on' on 13 Jan, which is slightly inconsistent with the other sources saying it was already issued that day.
b_issues: B includes an irrelevant Federal Reserve speeches page and a non-authoritative LinkedIn comment with opinionated, unverifiable claims about BoE/ECB QE losses. It also repeats the same '10 governors' figure from the Guardian excerpt, which conflicts with the BBC's claim of '11 senior bankers' within the same result set.
reasoning: Both sets reproduce the Guardian's 'solidarity statement' framing and share the same 10-vs-11 signatory discrepancy, so neither is perfectly clean. However, B includes the exact same URL as the anchor (the official ECB press release), giving direct confirmation of the authoritative source, and its BBC excerpt supplies additional verified statement text ('he is a respected colleague...') plus important context (the criminal investigation into Powell, reactions from Senator Tillis and former Fed chairs) that deepens and clarifies the answer beyond what A offers. A's content, while multi-sourced, is mostly redundant secondary reporting without reaching the primary/official statement text as directly as B does.
